rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA OSALVO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (761 Rolph St, San Francisco, Ca 94112)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) No proposed order submitted. (2) The purchase price ($880,000) does not meet 90% ($882,000) of the appraised value of the property ($980,000). Prob. Code sec. 10309. (3) Proof of publication of notice of sale. Prob. Code sec. 10300. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF TAI BING CHIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The account does not comply with the requirements in Probate Code Sections 1061 though 1064. 2) GC-400 is a mandatory form and must be attached to the accounting. 2) For the first account, all fiduciaries must also file original account statements showing the account balance on the date immediately preceding the date the fiduciary was appointed (Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2) and LRSF 14.34.D.1). 3) The beginning values of the attached account are different from the inventory and appraisal on file with the court. The account period must begin on the date petitioner was appointed as temporary conservator. 4) The instant petition requests distribution pursuant to the settlement agreement. Please clarify whether any substituted judgment trust was ever created. 5) Please review LRSF 14.47.F.3 for additional requirements for final accounts where the conservatorship has been terminated by the death of conservatee. 6) No proposed order has been reviewed or received by the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than December 1, 2025, to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORLITA O. DAMASCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet, as ordered on 7/22/24 and as required by Probate Code Sect. 12200. Letters were not issued until 11/8/24, but the administrator has not yet filed an I&A. [Prob. C. Sect. 8800(b).] No petition to terminate for lack of assets has been filed either. [Probate Code Section 12251.] The four other heirs have a right to know that they may petition for an accounting. The initial petition never set forth how the decedent's siblings are the heirs; her parents and their dates of death were not set forth, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.3 and 14.18 D.1. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HUI FAMILY SURVIVOR'S TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Invalidating The Hui Family Trust Dated Sept 15, 1992 & The Will Of Mr. Gordon Yue-Kwan Hui California Probate Code Section 21610 And
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (2) If an interested party is represented by an attorney, notice must be sent to the party and ALSO to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. (3) If the trustee does not reside (and administer the trust) in San Francisco, San Francisco is not the proper venue. [Probate Code Section 17005.] (4) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (5) It is not clear if the proper form of notice was served. The authority cited in the petition caption would require a JC form DE-120, not DE-115; however, the prayer also appears to be inconsistent with the caption. Is the petitioner seeking an order invalidating or modifying the trust or for distribution from the trust? (6) If this is a petition filed under Probate Code Section 850, a copy o f the petition must be served with the notice. (See item 5 of the proof of service and Probate Code Section 851(a).) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/12/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Petitioner, if she is in fact a surviving spouse, might wish to hire an attorney.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DORIS JOHANNA MEHLER REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 17, 2024
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Property As Trust Assets (Probate Code 17200.1b, 17206 And 850 Ct. Seq.) (Estate Of Heggstad)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The purported trustee resides in Millbrae. How is San Francisco the proper venue? (2) Did Nestor Bradley consent to or request electronic service of notice? [CRC 2.251(b).] (3) Prob. C. Sect. 850 is a shortcut for a formal probate proceeding. A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Who are the grantor's heirs-at-law? (4) The trust has handwritten interlineations, but the petitioner does not address these. Some of them appear to be inconsistent: three beneficiaries were crossed out in item 4a of the trust, but two of them (Kyle Goldman and Birgit Hoffman) are still included in subparagraphs 13w - z. (5) Paragraph 12 of the petition includes all the beneficiaries named in the trust, including those whose names were crossed out by hand, but Petitioner claims that she is the acting successor trustee, when her name had been interlineated into the trust. (6) Copies of the vesting deed and bank or financial institution statements immediately preceding the grantor's date of death must be filed to show how title to the properties and accounts was held. (7) Only specific assets will be confirmed as assets of a trust via a petition filed under Probate Code Section 850, not "all interest, including," etc. (8) The notice must clearly identify the assets, including the account numbers or shares of securities. If this is, indeed, the proper venue, please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/12/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN VANCE EVANS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the whereabouts of interested parties are unknown, a declaration must be filed describing efforts made to locate the persons entitled to notice. If, after diligent search, such persons cannot be located, an ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice to such persons be dispensed with. (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) If notice is required, notice must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: A proposed order must be prepared on the assumption that the petition will be granted; i.e., with the appropriate findings and orders completed on the JC form.)
